sanskrit:
ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते।
सङ्गात्सञ्जायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते॥
transliteration: dhyāyato viṣayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣūpajāyate | saṅgāt sañjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho ’bhijāyate ||
literal_meaning: While contemplating sense-objects, attachment arises in a person; from attachment, desire is born; from desire, anger manifests.
contextual_meaning: A micro-map of how unguarded attention snowballs into destructive emotion: attention → attachment → craving → rage.
psychological_state: Pre-addictive loop: repeated mental rehearsal of a reward sensitizes the limbic system, narrowing perception until frustration flips into aggression.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling late-night Instagram reels; each swipe deepens the hook, the feed stops loading, and you hurl the phone at the wall.
anchor_text: Watch your thoughts; unchecked, they become wants, then wrath.
